title: Starstorm One
content:
{{Top|fa|leg|italics=1}} {{IndividualShip |image=[[File:StarstormOne-TotJTSW5.jpg]] |name=''Starstorm One'' |designer= |manufacturer= |line= |model= |type= |cost= |modifier= |sysmods= |length= |width= |height= |mass= |max accel= |mglt= |max speed= |maneuverability= |engine=3[[Tales of the Jedi – The Sith War 5|''Tales of the Jedi – The Sith War'' 5]] |hyperdrive=Equipped[[Tales of the Jedi – Dark Lords of the Sith 2|''Tales of the Jedi – Dark Lords of the Sith'' 2]] |hdrange= |hdsystem= |poweroutput= |power= |shield gen= |hull= |sensor= |target= |navigation=[[Navigation computer/Legends|Navigation computer]][[Tales of the Jedi: Dark Lords of the Sith (audio drama)|''Tales of the Jedi: Dark Lords of the Sith'' audio drama]] |avionics= |maincomp= |countermeasures= |armament= |complement= |bays= |escapepods= |crew= |skeleton=1 |passengers=Up to 2 |capacity= |cargohandling= |consumables= |lifesupport= |communications= |othersystems= |role=Personal [[Starship/Legends|starship]] |commission= |destroyed= |retired= |battles=[[Battle of Ossus (Great Sith War)|Battle of Ossus]] |affiliation=*[[Galactic Republic/Legends|Galactic Republic]] *[[Jedi Order/Legends|Jedi Order]] *[[Sith Empire (Exar Kun)/Legends|Exar Kun's Sith Empire]] |navy= |fleet= |owners=[[Exar Kun/Legends|Exar Kun]] |captains= |crewmembers= |registry= |aliases= }} {{Quote|Computer, new heading. Maximum speed. Set course for Korriban.|Exar Kun, directing ''Starstorm One'' to fly to Korriban|}} '''''Starstorm One''''' was the personal [[Starship/Legends|starship]] of [[Jedi Knight/Legends|Jedi Knight]] and later [[Dark Lord of the Sith/Legends|Dark Lord of the Sith]] [[Exar Kun/Legends|Exar Kun]] during the [[Old Sith Wars|period]] of the [[Great Sith War/Legends|Great Sith War]]. A curious young [[Gender/Legends|man]] who was fascinated by the ancient [[Force power/Legends|powers]] of the [[Sith/Legends|Sith]], Kun set out in ''Starstorm One'' on a quest to learn forbidden secrets in [[3997 BBY]]. His search led him and ''Starstorm One'' to [[Onderon/Legends|Onderon]], where he found two [[Dark side of the Force/Legends|dark side]] [[Sith cult/Legends|cultists]] to guide him to the [[Tomb of Freedon Nadd|tomb]] of the [[Death/Legends|dead]] Dark [[Lord/Legends|Lord]] of the Sith [[Freedon Nadd]]. The [[Naddist|cultists]], [[Nebo]] and [[Rask]], traveled aboard ''Starstorm One'' and directed him to the tomb, where Kun found two [[Sith scroll/Legends|Sith scrolls]] that gave him directions to the Sith [[Homeworld/Legends|homeworld]], [[Korriban/Legends|Korriban]]. Kun flew the ship to the lost [[Planet/Legends|planet]], where the [[Force ghost/Legends|spirit]] of Nadd converted Kun to the dark side. Nadd directed Kun to fly ''Starstorm One'' to the [[Moon/Legends|moon]] of [[Yavin 4/Legends|Yavin 4]] to continue his [[Sith training]] at the place where Dark Lord [[Naga Sadow/Legends|Naga Sadow]] had experimented in [[Sith alchemy/Legends|Sith alchemy]] a [[5000 BBY/Legends|thousand years before]]. Upon landing on Yavin 4, ''Starstorm One'' was destroyed by primitive [[Massassi/Legends|Massassi]] warriors, the descendants of Sadow's followers. At some point over the next [[Standard year/Legends|year]], the ship was repaired, however; by that [[Time/Legends|time]] Kun was a full-fledged Dark Lord of the Sith and had launched a war against the [[Galactic Republic/Legends|Galactic Republic]] and the [[Jedi Order/Legends|Jedi Order]]. ''Starstorm One'' returned to Kun's service and flew a group of [[Jedi/Legends|Jedi]] Knights from [[Ossus/Legends|Ossus]] to Yavin 4, where they were converted to the dark side by Kun. Later that year, after a [[Cron Supernova/Legends|supernova]] prompted an [[Battle of Ossus (Great Sith War)|evacuation]] of Ossus and its [[Great Jedi Library/Legends|Great Jedi Library]], ''Starstorm One'' was used to [[Transport/Legends|transport]] as many stolen [[Jedi artifact/Legends|Jedi artifacts]] as Kun's ship was able to carry. He returned on ''Starstorm One'' once more to Yavin 4, where he used [[Sith sorcery/Legends|Sith magic]] to [[Transfer essence|shed]] his mortal body to flee Jedi pursuers. ==Description== ''Starstorm One'' had a rather unorthodox appearance; in flight, the [[Starship/Legends|ship]] resembled the [[Aurebesh/Legends|Aurebesh]] letter [[Trill/Legends|Trill]] and consisted of one straight structure with two perpendicular wings extending from the tip that were curved in relation to the main mass of the vessel. When the ship landed, the different protrusions folded on top of one another. Although the ship could be operated by just a single [[Pilot/Legends|pilot]], the [[Cockpit/Legends|cockpit]], located at its center, was able to hold as many as three [[Humanoid/Legends|humanoids]]. The entire ship could hold many more—in [[3996 BBY]], it held an entire [[Battalion/Legends|battalion]] of [[Massassi/Legends|Massassi]] warriors. Within the cockpit, a joystick controlled the ship, with several [[Viewport/Legends|viewports]] that surrounded the pilot, and a [[Targeting computer/Legends|targeting computer]] located behind the control stick. ''Starstorm One'' had significant cargo space and was able to hold hoards of [[Jedi/Legends|Jedi]] [[Jedi artifact/Legends|treasures]] looted from the [[Great Jedi Library/Legends|Great Jedi Library]] in 3996 BBY. [[Hyperdrive/Legends|Hyperdrive]]-equipped, the ship had three [[Engine/Legends|engines]] located along its main straight mass. ''Starstorm One'' contained an [[Electronic translator/Legends|electronic translator]], capable of translating even the ancient [[Sith (language)|Sith]]; the translator also doubled as the ship's [[Navigation computer/Legends|navigation computer]]. Voice-activated, the device displayed information about different [[Planet/Legends|planets]] on command. ==History== ===Kun's quest=== {{Quote|Make your ship ready.''"
"''Yes. I ''am'' going to Yavin Four. But unlike you, I will ''never'' be seduced by the dark side.|Freedon Nadd's spirit and Exar Kun|[[Tales of the Jedi – Dark Lords of the Sith 3|''Tales of the Jedi – Dark Lords of the Sith'' 3]]}} [[File:StarstormOne-TotJTSW4.jpg|thumb|left|''Starstorm One'' arrives on Yavin 4.]] ''Starstorm One'' was the personal starship of the [[Human/Legends|Human]] [[Gender/Legends|male]] [[Jedi Knight/Legends|Jedi Knight]] [[Exar Kun/Legends|Exar Kun]] during the [[Old Sith Wars|era]] of the [[Great Sith War/Legends|Great Sith War]]. Kun became interested in the [[Dark side of the Force/Legends|dark side of the Force]], and, in [[3997 BBY]], he began a quest to learn the forbidden mysteries of the [[Sith/Legends|Sith]]. Kun soon learned of a trove of [[Sith artifact/Legends|Sith artifacts]] discovered in the [[Ommin's subterranean fortress|lair]] of [[darksider]] [[King/Legends|King]] [[Ommin/Legends|Ommin]] of [[Onderon/Legends|Onderon]] and set out in ''Starstorm One'' to learn about them. Posing as a [[Jedi archaeologist]], Kun landed ''Starstorm One'' on a landing pad near a derelict [[Freedon Nadd's starship|starship]] being converted into a [[Jedi/Legends|Jedi]] outpost and met with the Jedi [[Cay Qel-Droma]] and [[Tott Doneeta]], who had helped to find the artifacts. However, [[Jedi Master/Legends|Jedi Master]] [[Arca Jeth]] discerned that Kun had lied about his occupation as an [[Archaeology/Legends|archaeologist]] and recognized his fascination with the dark side, and Kun was barred from examining the artifacts. Kun left in a huff, flying ''Starstorm One'' to the Onderonian [[Capital/Legends|capital]] [[City/Legends|city]] of [[Iziz/Legends|Iziz]]. Kun wanted to learn more about Onderon's dark side past and soon found two dark side [[Sith cult/Legends|cultists]], [[Nebo]] and [[Rask]], to fly him to the [[Tomb of Freedon Nadd|tomb]] of former [[Dark Lord of the Sith/Legends|Dark Lord of the Sith]] [[Freedon Nadd]] on the [[Moon/Legends|moon]] [[Dxun/Legends|Dxun]]. The three returned to ''Starstorm One'', and Kun's new associates guided him to the site of the mausoleum. Kun brought the ship down some distance away from the structure, breached its [[Beskar/Legends|Mandalorian iron]] walls, and found a [[Sith scroll/Legends|scroll]] within that contained the location of the Sith [[Homeworld/Legends|homeworld]], [[Korriban/Legends|Korriban]]. Nebo and Rask attacked Kun upon his emergence from the tomb, as the two zealots thought him a tomb desecrator, but Kun swiftly [[Death/Legends|killed]] them both and departed the moon in ''Starstorm One''. Using the ship's electronic translator to examine the scrolls, Kun followed their directions to Korriban. Kun landed ''Starstorm One'' on a fallen column at the entrance to Korriban's fabled [[Valley of the Dark Lords/Legends|Valley of the Dark Lords]], where the ancient Dark [[Lord/Legends|Lords]] of the Sith were buried. Deep within the Valley, Kun was turned to the dark side of [[The Force/Legends|the Force]] by the [[Force ghost/Legends|spirit]] of Freedon Nadd and was told to prepare ''Starstorm One'' and travel to the moon [[Yavin 4/Legends|Yavin 4]], a place with a long Sith history, to complete his [[Sith training|dark side training]]. Yavin 4 was the place where the ancient Dark Lord [[Naga Sadow/Legends|Naga Sadow]] fled with his followers after being [[Second battle of Korriban|attacked]] by the [[Galactic Republic/Legends|Republic]] during the [[Great Hyperspace War/Legends|Great Hyperspace War]],[[Tales of the Jedi – The Fall of the Sith Empire 5|''Tales of the Jedi – The Fall of the Sith Empire'' 5]] and, although he was long dead, he left behind the primitive descendants of his Massassi followers and the laboratories of his [[Sith alchemy/Legends|Sith alchemy]]. After a journey in [[Hyperspace/Legends|hyperspace]], ''Starstorm One'' reached Yavin 4 and was brought to rest in a clearing in the moon's forests. As Kun left the ship to explore the Yavin 4 wilderness, the Massassi attacked him. Kun tried his best to fend off his attackers, but dozens of Massassi swarmed over ''Starstorm One'' and stripped it of brightly [[Color/Legends|colored]] parts to use as bodily decorations. The ship was rendered inoperable as a result, but Kun was able to survive and use his newfound dark power to establish dominion over the Massassi.[[Tales of the Jedi – Dark Lords of the Sith 4|''Tales of the Jedi – Dark Lords of the Sith'' 4]] ===Wartime missions=== {{Quote|We have arrived, Exar Kun.''"
"''Yes, Ulic—Ossus is ours for the taking.|Ulic Qel-Droma and Exar Kun|}} [[File:StarstormOneAboveOssus.jpg|thumb|right|300px|''Starstorm One'' hovers above Ossus, as the Cron Supernova threatens to destroy the planet.]] Despite its destruction by the Massassi upon landing on Yavin 4, ''Starstorm One'' was eventually repaired and returned to the service of Kun. In the meanwhile, Kun had ascended to the title of Dark Lord of the Sith and taken Jedi Knight [[Ulic Qel-Droma/Legends|Ulic Qel-Droma]] as his [[Sith apprentice/Legends|apprentice]]. Together, and with an [[Sith Empire (Exar Kun)/Legends|army]] of Massassi and [[Mandalorian/Legends|Mandalorian]] warriors that had pledged service to their cause, they began a war against the Republic and the Jedi Knights. In 3,996 BBY, Kun flew ''Starstorm One'' from Yavin 4 to the Jedi world of [[Ossus/Legends|Ossus]], hoping to recruit other Jedi to his budding Sith movement. Withholding his status as a [[Sith Lord/Legends|Sith Lord]], he lured several Knights to his cause by promising to teach them powerful [[Force power/Legends|Force techniques]] forbidden by the Masters of the Order. Kun brought the Jedi to Yavin 4 onboard ''Starstorm One'', intending to turn them to the dark side. The wayward Jedi Knights were successfully seduced by the Dark Lord and possessed with the spirits contained inside an [[Dark Holocron|ancient Sith holocron]]. Kun then gave the new [[Sith acolyte/Legends|Sith acolytes]] a [[Sith uprising|mission]] to kill their Jedi Masters.[[Tales of the Jedi – The Sith War 2|''Tales of the Jedi – The Sith War'' 2]] Later in the war, ''Starstorm One'' was called into action yet again. During a [[Battle of Kemplex Nine|battle]] between the Sith and the Republic at [[Kemplex Nine]], the use of [[Sith sorcery/Legends|Sith magic]] caused a [[Cron Supernova/Legends|supernova]] in the [[Cron Cluster/Legends|Cron Cluster]] [[Star/Legends|star]] group. The shock wave headed directly toward Ossus, which contained countless Jedi artifacts and the greatest repository of Jedi knowledge in [[The galaxy/Legends|the galaxy]], the Great Jedi Library. Seeing his chance to raid the planet of its treasures, Kun set out for Ossus in ''Starstorm One'', with Qel-Droma accompanying him in his own [[Cay's Dream|ship]]. They arrived during a large-scale [[Battle of Ossus (Great Sith War)|evacuation]] of the planet, as thousands tried to flee while the Jedi below attempted to save as much precious knowledge as they could. Kun and Qel-Droma were flanked by a large Mandalorian assault force, which created havoc while the two Sith Lords sneaked down to the planet's surface and began their treasure hunt.[[Tales of the Jedi – The Sith War 6|''Tales of the Jedi – The Sith War'' 6]] Kun landed ''Starstorm One'' in a busy Ossus square, and he and the Massassi guards he brought with him searched the Great Jedi Library, taking whatever they could find and loading ''Starstorm One'' with stolen artifacts. His job done, Kun departed the doomed planet in his ship and returned to Yavin 4 to study his new possessions. Kun was followed by a large group of Jedi, and, knowing he could not defeat the large number of assembled Knights, Kun decided to use ancient Sith magic to shed his mortal body and become a Force spirit. However, although he succeeded in leaving his body behind, he was [[Transfer essence|trapped]] inside his [[Temple of Exar Kun|temple]] for [[11 ABY/Legends|thousands of years]].[[Dark Apprentice (novel)|''Dark Apprentice'']] ==Commanders and crew== ''Starstorm One'' was the personal starship of Exar Kun, who used it during his quest to discover the mysteries of the Sith and also during the Great Sith War. He usually flew the ship alone, but not always; Nebo and Rask were used aboard the ship as guides during Kun's search for Freedon Nadd's temple. A number of Massassi also served aboard the ship during the Great Sith War. Although ''Starstorm One'' was destroyed during Kun's tenure as the ship's owner by the Massassi on Yavin 4, he eventually had it repaired. ==Behind the scenes== ''Starstorm One'' first appeared in [[Star Wars: Tales of the Jedi – Dark Lords of the Sith|''Dark Lords of the Sith'']], a story arc of the [[Star Wars: Tales of the Jedi (comic series)|''Tales of the Jedi'']] [[Star Wars comic books|comic book]] series, written by [[Tom Veitch]] and [[Kevin J. Anderson]], and released from late [[1994]] to early [[1995]]. ''Starstorm One'' appeared in the [[Tales of the Jedi – Dark Lords of the Sith 2|second]], [[Tales of the Jedi – Dark Lords of the Sith 3|third]], and [[Tales of the Jedi – Dark Lords of the Sith 4|fourth]] issues, and was also mentioned in the [[Tales of the Jedi – Dark Lords of the Sith 5|fifth]].[[Tales of the Jedi – Dark Lords of the Sith 5|''Tales of the Jedi – Dark Lords of the Sith'' 5]] The ship also appeared in the [[Tales of the Jedi – The Sith War 2|second]] and [[Tales of the Jedi – The Sith War 5|fifth]] issues of ''Dark Lords of the Sith''{{'s}} sequel arc, [[Star Wars: Tales of the Jedi – The Sith War|''The Sith War'']]. During its appearances in ''Tales of the Jedi'', ''Starstorm One'' was illustrated by two different pencillers, [[Chris Gossett]] and [[Dario Carrasco, Jr.]] ''Starstorm One'' additionally appeared in the [[Tales of the Jedi: Dark Lords of the Sith (audio drama)|''Dark Lords of the Sith'' audio adaptation]]. ==Appearances== {{Mediacat|imagecat=Images of Starstorm One}} *[[Tales of the Jedi: Dark Lords of the Sith (audio drama)|''Tales of the Jedi: Dark Lords of the Sith'' audio drama]] *[[Tales of the Jedi – Dark Lords of the Sith 2|''Tales of the Jedi – Dark Lords of the Sith'' 2]] {{1st}} *[[Tales of the Jedi – Dark Lords of the Sith 3|''Tales of the Jedi – Dark Lords of the Sith'' 3]] *[[Tales of the Jedi – Dark Lords of the Sith 4|''Tales of the Jedi – Dark Lords of the Sith'' 4]] *[[Tales of the Jedi – Dark Lords of the Sith 5|''Tales of the Jedi – Dark Lords of the Sith'' 5]] {{Mo}} *[[Tales of the Jedi – The Sith War 2|''Tales of the Jedi – The Sith War'' 2]] *[[Tales of the Jedi – The Sith War 5|''Tales of the Jedi – The Sith War'' 5]] ==Sources== *{{InsiderCite|28|The Horse's Mouth|Straight from the Horse's Mouth: A Guide to the ''Star Wars: Tales of the Jedi'' Universe, Part 3}} *''[[Star Wars Encyclopedia]]'' *''[[A Guide to the Star Wars Universe, Third Edition, Revised and Expanded]]'' *''[[The Complete Star Wars Encyclopedia]]'' {{ID|''Starstorm I''}} ==Notes and references== {{Reflist}} {{Interlang |de=Starstorm Eins |es=Tormenta Estelar Uno }} [[Category:Starships of Exar Kun's Sith Empire]] [[Category:Starships of the Galactic Republic]] [[Category:Starships of the Jedi Order]]
